title: Balanophora sphaerica
taxonomy: Balanophora sphaerica is a species of parasitic plant in the family Balanophoraceae. This family is part of the order Santalales, which is a group of parasitic plants that obtain their nutrients from other organisms. Balanophora sphaerica is native to Southeast Asia and is known for its unique, spherical shape. The plant is classified as a holoparasite, meaning it has lost the ability to photosynthesize and relies on other plants for its nutritional needs. Balanophora sphaerica is considered a rare and unusual species due to its parasitic nature and limited geographic distribution.

intro: Balanophora sphaerica, commonly known as the "dwarf balanophora," is a type of parasitic plant that belongs to the family Balanophoraceae. The plant is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Balanophora sphaerica include balanophorin, balanophorin A, and other alkaloids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Balanophora sphaerica is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, coughs, and skin conditions.
